title: Monitoring and Measuring Procedure
preamble: This procedure defines requirements for monitoring and measuring HSESC performance across the owner-led project to verify control effectiveness, confirm compliance with performance criteria, detect changing conditions, and support adaptive management and continuous improvement.
owns: HSESC monitoring programme scope and frequencies
Inspection programme (CCFVs, PTW checks, proof tests)
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0002 – Risk Management (risk register as basis for monitoring programme)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0009 – Incident Management (incident data as monitoring input)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0007 – Improvement Planning (monitoring data as improvement input)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0044 – Auditing (audits as part of performance assessment)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0034, RTPR-HSE-PRO-0035, RTPR-HSE-PRO-0038 – Environmental procedures (environmental monitoring)
referenced_by: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0007 references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0042 for performance data
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0044 references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0042 for the monitoring programme being audited

requirements.text: Define the scope of the project HSESC monitoring programme covering: safety performance leading and lagging indicators; occupational health and hygiene monitoring; environmental monitoring (water, air, soil, biodiversity); CRM verification performance; SMM maturity assessment; and social and community performance indicators.

Define monitoring frequency, methodology, QA/QC requirements, responsible roles, and data recording requirements for each monitoring type. Require monitoring data to be analysed and interpreted at least annually, or at a frequency suited to operational needs and regulatory requirements.

requirements.tag: [TSM – TSM – Safe, Healthy and Respectful Workplaces Protocol, Indicator SH2]
requirements.text: Set and monitor measurable safety and health targets, reporting performance against them as part of the monthly and annual HSESC performance reporting cycle.

Define the planned HSESC inspection programme covering: site inspections; equipment inspections; CRM critical control field verifications (CCFVs); PTW system compliance checks; and process safety proof tests. Define inspection frequency, required competency of inspectors, and documentation requirements.
Conduct regular workplace inspections to identify new or recurring hazards, and document findings in the project action tracking system with assigned corrective actions.

Require all safety-critical measuring and monitoring equipment to be calibrated per manufacturer requirements, with calibration records maintained. Require gas detector bump-testing before each use with results recorded on relevant permits.

Ensure that environmental monitoring uses methods consistent with nationally or internationally recognised analytical and sampling standards, and that data quality is verified through QA/QC processes.

Require Trigger Action Response Plans (TARPs) for all material environmental and safety risks, specifying: trigger level; responsible role; required action; escalation path; and response timeframe. Require TARPs to be tested through drills and exercises at defined intervals.
Ensure that adaptive management responses to monitoring trigger events are implemented promptly and that the effectiveness of corrective actions is verified and recorded.

project_completion_guidance: Develop the project HSESC monitoring programme as a schedule: list each monitoring type; define frequency; assign responsible roles; define the data recording method. Insert as Appendix A.
Define the project monthly HSESC report template, aligned to the RT Appendix C-8 template requirements. The procedure defines content — the project produces the formatted template.
Define the project gas detector calibration register and bump-test recording system. The procedure requires calibration records — define the project-specific record format and storage.
Develop the project TARPs for all material environmental and safety risks identified in RTPR-HSE-PRO-0002. Each TARP must be specific to this project's monitoring locations and trigger thresholds.

[APPC – Appendix C, C-1 §40.1.1]  Require that the project makes all reasonable efforts to accommodate Company HSESC audits and provides unrestricted access to facilities, equipment, materials, personnel, and records. Company audits will be conducted with a minimum of two working days notice.

[APPC – Appendix C, C-1 §40.2.1]  Require that instructions from the Company based on inspection findings are implemented within a mutually agreed timeframe. Inspection outcomes must be recorded and corrective actions tracked to ensure timely resolution.

[APPC – Appendix C, C-1 §41.3.1]  Require that HSESC metrics including objectives, targets, and KPIs are proposed by the Contractor and agreed with the Company before execution phase commences. Agreed metrics must be reported at agreed intervals.
Define the project CRM verification schedule: frequency of CCFVs by critical risk; target completion rate; reporting format to the project owner.

external_sources.summary: Requires definition of the HSESC monitoring programme scope covering safety, health, environmental, CRM, SMM and social performance indicators.

external_sources.clause: RT Environment and Nature – General Requirements Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires Trigger Action Response Plans for all material environmental and safety risks with defined trigger levels, actions, escalation paths and response timeframes.

external_sources.clause: RT C5 – Confined Space Standard / RT C2 – Electrical Safety Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires all safety-critical measuring and monitoring equipment to be calibrated per manufacturer requirements and gas detectors to be bump-tested before each use.
Requires a planned HSESC inspection programme covering site inspections, equipment inspections, CCFVs, PTW compliance checks and process safety proof tests.
Requires monitoring frequency, methodology, QA/QC, responsible roles and data recording to be defined, with monitoring data analysed and interpreted at least annually.
Requires adaptive management responses to monitoring trigger events to be implemented promptly and the effectiveness of corrective actions to be verified and recorded.
Requires environmental monitoring to use nationally or internationally recognised analytical and sampling standards with data quality verified through QA/QC processes.

external_sources.clause: TSM – Safe, Healthy and Respectful Workplaces Protocol, Indicator SH2
external_sources.summary: Requires measurable safety and health targets to be set and monitored, with performance reported as part of the monthly and annual HSESC performance reporting cycle.

external_sources.clause: OSHA RP 2016 – Hazard Identification and Assessment
external_sources.summary: Requires regular workplace inspections to identify new or recurring hazards with findings documented in the project action tracking system.
